DISCOVERY, 302, PLAINTIFFS CHRISTIAN MALFILATRE AND VICTOR HERNANDEZ'S (COLLECTIVELY "MALFILATRE & HERNANDEZ'S"), MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSE TO INTERROGATORY NO. 2, IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS SHALL RESPOND TO THIS INTERROGATORY WITHIN (1) ONE WEEK OF THE COURT'S ORDER. MALFILATRE & HERNANDEZ'S REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN AN AMOUNT OF $3150 AGAINST DEFENDANT NOT COUNSEL. SANCTIONS TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF THE COURT'S ORDER. ALL OTHER REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS, IF ANY, ARE DENIED. (SEE WORD MINUTES AND FORMAL ORDER FOR COMPLETE RULING.) APPROVED-AS-TO-FORM ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM: DOUGLAS ROBBINS, CLERK: SEAN KANE, NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT SERGE COLOMBEAU'S (COLOMBEAU)'S MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RESPONSES AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED ON THE GROUNDS THAT COLOMBEAU'S DISCOVERY REQUESTS ARE UNTIMELY. ALL DISCOVERY PROCEEDINGS UNDER THE CALIFORNIA DISCOVERY ACT MUST BE COMPLETED ON OR BEFORE THE 30TH DAY BEFORE THE DATE INITIALLY SET FOR TRIAL, IRRESPECTIVE OF CONTINUANCES OR POSTPONEMENTS OF THAT TRIAL DATE, UNLESS AN EXCEPTION APPLIES. CCP SECTION 2024.020. THIRTY DAYS MEANS THIRTY CALENDAR DAYS. THE "ON OR BEFORE" WORDING OF CCP SECTION 2024.020(A) PRECLUDES EXTENDING THE CUT-OFF DATE IF THE 30TH DAY FALLS ON A SATURDAY OR HOLIDAY. CCP SECTIONS 2016.060 AND 2024.020 DO NOT ALTER THE PLAIN LANGUAGE OF, OR THE LEGISLATIVE INTENT BEHIND, CCP SECTION 2024.020. STEELE V. BARTLETT (1941) 18 CAL. 2D 573, 574, 116 P. 2D 780. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE PRO-TEM; MARK LEHOCKY; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED. =302/JPT

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT SERGE COLOMBEAU'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION AND SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION ALLEGES SUFFICIENT FACTS TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF CIVIL CODE SECTION 52.1. (FAC (FAC 15, 24, 39.) PLAINTIFFS CORRECTLY NOTE THAT TO ALLEGE A CAUSE OF ACTION UNDER CIVIL CODE SECTION 52.1, A PLAINTIFF IS NOT REQUIRED TO PLEAD FACTS STATING THAT THE INTERFERENCE WAS DUE TO PLAINTIFF'S RACE, COLOR, RELIGION, ANCESTRY, NATIONAL ORIGIN, POLITICAL AFFILIATION, SEX, SEXUAL ORIENTATION, AGE, DISABILITY, OR POSITION IN A LABOR DISPUTE, AS SET FORTH IN CIVIL CODE SECTION 51.7. (VENEGAS V. COUNTY OF LOS ANGELES (2004) 32 CAL.4TH 820, 842.) AS NOW PLED, THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION DOES NOT SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGE ULTIMATE FACTS TO STATE A CLAIM FOR VIOLATION OF SECTION 51.7. THE ALLEGATION IN PARAGRAPH 41THAT THE "ACTS OR THREATS WERE SPECIFICALLY BASED ON HERNANDEZ' ANCESTRY/NATIONAL ORIGIN" IS A CONCLUSION. PLAINTIFF HERNANDEZ IS GIVEN THE OPPORTUNITY, IF HE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, TO ALLEGE IN A SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT THE FACTS STATED AT LINES 3-8 ON PAGE 8 OF HIS OPPOSITION BRIEF. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PROVIDE A FORM OF ORDER AND COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489. (302/HK)

Off Calendar Nov-04-2016 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jan-24-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-01-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The court adopted its tentative ruling as followsa;
Defendant Serge Colombeau's (Colombeau)'s Motion To Compel Discovery Responses and Request For Sanctions is Denied on the grounds that Colombeau's discovery requests are untimely. All discovery proceedings under the California Discovery Act must be completed on or before the 30th day before the date initially set for trial, irrespective of continuances or postponements of that trial date, unless an exception applies. CCP Section 2024.020. Thirty days means thirty calendar days. The "on or before" wording of CCP Section 2024.020(a) precludes extending the cut-off date if the 30th day falls on a Saturday or holiday. CCP Sections 2016.060 and 2024.020 do not alter the plain language of, or the legislative intent behind, CCP Section 2024.020. Steele v. Bartlett (1941) 18 Cal. 2d 573, 574, 116 P. 2d 780. Prevailing party to prepare form of order. Judge Pro-tem; Mark Lehocky; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not Reported. =302/JPT

MINI MINUTES:

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-14-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Defendant Serge Colombeau's Demurrer to Plaintiffs' 1ST Amended Complaint is overruled as to the fifth cause of action and sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the sixth cause of action The fifth cause of action alleges sufficient facts to state a cause of action for violation of Civil Code section 52.1. (FAC (FAC 15, 24, 39.) Plaintiffs correctly note that to allege a cause of action under Civil Code section 52.1, a plaintiff is not required to plead facts stating that the interference was due to plaintiff's race, color, religion, ancestry, national origin, political affiliation, sex, sexual orientation, age, disability, or position in a labor dispute, as set forth in Civil Code section 51.7. (Venegas v. County of Los Angeles (2004) 32 Cal.4th 820, 842.) As now pled, the sixth cause of action does not sufficiently allege ultimate facts to state a claim for violation of section 51.7. The allegation in paragraph 41that the "acts or threats were specifically based on Hernandez' ancestry/national origin" is a conclusion. Plaintiff Hernandez is given the opportunity, if he can do so in good faith, to allege in a second amended complaint the facts stated at lines 3-8 on page 8 of his opposition brief. The prevailing party is to provide a form of order and comply with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489. (302/HK)
